title: The symbolism of Toujis head carry backpack
display_title: The symbolism of Toujis head carry backpack
wikitext:
[]: The symbolism of Touji´s head-carry backpack.             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sun Nov 28, 2010 6:49 am I just saw this on /a/ and I thought that it might be interesting. is this guy carrying his backpack using his head? wtf, why does he do this

[]:             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Sun Nov 28, 2010 7:28 am i think hes just a goofy kid who says back packs are for norms, every one knows head packs are for real hardcore people...thats just me though but on a serious note i dont really see the tentacle referance, looks just like a bag strap thats going across some ones head... my other first opinion is that when i first saw the first quote that said why is he wearing the bag on his head, my first thought was "well its obviosuly symbolism of the weight he carries while taking care of his sister." so ima go ahead and say that one

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Nov 28, 2010 7:52 am It's just because he's Toji. Doesn't mean anything.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Nov 28, 2010 8:11 am It's a striking personality trait so they put it in.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 28, 2010 8:32 am /ɐ/non is even more a fanwanker than any of us. /ɐ/non may be among us, but keeping that side of themselves hidden.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Nov 28, 2010 9:47 am Brilliant. Also, Alien Nine much?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sun Nov 28, 2010 9:52 am Sometimes a backpack is just a backpack.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sun Nov 28, 2010 10:51 am ^ Not in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Nov 28, 2010 11:24 am Toji wearing his backpack on his head as well as Kenuke's glasses symbolize the same thing. Teenage lust toward the opposite sex.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun Nov 28, 2010 11:54 am Toji wearing his backpack on his head implies that he was regularly sleeping with Ritsuko. Once you make the obvious 'white backpack strap --> white labcoat' connection, the innuendo is pretty clear.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:09 pm Merridian wrote: Toji wearing his backpack on his head implies that he was regularly sleeping with Ritsuko. Once you make the obvious 'white backpack strap --> white labcoat' connection, the innuendo is pretty clear.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:14 pm Uhhhhh......

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun Nov 28, 2010 12:50 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Uhhhhh......

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Nov 28, 2010 1:00 pm It symbolizes sexual inhibition and frustration typical for a male adolescent. The white strap-on alludes to traditional Japanese male underwear (fundoshi) and his two tear streams to semen. He can't remove his "fundoshi" (clothes, societal norms, age gap) and have sex with Misato

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Nov 28, 2010 1:08 pm Toji's head pack is a trail of popcorn leading us to further information with which we shall eventually discover that he was raped for information, by Vincent Price's character in the tiki episode.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Sun Nov 28, 2010 1:30 pm The fanwank is strong with this thread, but seriously... No. No no no no no. The weight on his head symbolizes the deepest secret of all of evangelion: Toji is the Jesus. The band around his head is his crown of thorns. Instead of being crucified, he is mauled near death (in manga, completely to death) by the Judas (Shinji) who will be punished for his crime with eternal damnation in the world, being thrown away from the heaven. Just for the kicks, Jesus (Toji) threw 'Red Devil' in there too, to torment him. Too bad Toji had no idea who 'Red Devil' actually was in the inside...

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sun Nov 28, 2010 1:37 pm ^ If Toji´s Jesus, would that mean that Toji is/becomes Shinji´s son-in-law?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Nov 28, 2010 2:21 pm Allemann wrote: It symbolizes sexual inhibition and frustration typical for a male adolescent. The white strap-on alludes to traditional Japanese male underwear (fundoshi) and his two tear streams to semen. He can't remove his "fundoshi" (clothes, societal norms, age gap) and have sex with Misato

[]:             Quote Post by DoubleZeva » Sun Nov 28, 2010 3:57 pm He carries it like that because it's symbolic that "he thinks with his muscles." In laymans terms he's a Knucklehead.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Nov 28, 2010 6:02 pm Genius thread is genius

Article: The symbolism of Toujis head carry backpack
 the deepest secret of all of evangelion: Toji is the Jesus. The band around his head is his crown of thorns. Instead of being crucified, he is mauled near death (in manga, completely to death) by the Judas (Shinji) who will be punished for his crime with eternal damnation in the world, being thrown away from the heaven. Just for the kicks, Jesus (Toji) threw 'Red Devil' in there too, to torment him. Too bad Toji had no idea who 'Red Devil' actually was in the inside...
